Hi everyone! I recently came back to the game and have been struggling with team comps now that I’m on chapter 9 and hard stage chapter 5. Enemies hit like a truck and even using gnosis 3+ times hasn’t been working well <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>

fave team is usually gmurph, tulu, corp, and celeste, but I’ll also use tawill, nautila, Thais, and faint. I had a tawill + castor and but kept switching around the other awakeners but couldn’t find something I liked. If you can tell from my wheels, currently in wheel jail for a +12 blade <:pray:1356053116260454534> 

Any help is appreciated! Thank you!!
You probably do not want to run celest in a Gmurphy comp
If you want to run Gmurphy in arc 1
Gmurphy thais tulu +1 flex slot should work
Tbh I’ve heard celeste is not the best, but I was relying on her for heals <:bceFrownSad:593020248836538368> would corp or caecus work better?
Probably yea
Healing is not great in general(unless you are caro)

Corposant wants a Team of her own with Aurita & Cassiah ideally, and works as more of a Burst Turn gimmick with Lightning Retribution x4+ into Ultra Turn, shielding at adverse turns where you're likely to take heavy damage and attack scaling via Glands + Pilots
Caecus would work fine as a Healer just fine though, and he also doubles as a Shielder too
W Crimson Furnace
Ok good to know!! I guess I’m still struggling with being able to stack enough shield to not get KOd immediately 😭
but I’ll definitely try out that team! Thank you!!

Yeah, healing and shield are weird in this game because it *ain't* Slay the Spire.
Lategame, the enemies will kill you relatively quick, and it is mainly your job to kill them first. Good use of shielding/healing can buy you another 2-3 turns to kill them in, but you're still staring down the barrel of Death Resistance.
It's less "damage neutral" and more "HP is a timer that runs out slightly slower with good defensive tools"

to summarize, u want to build around a unit that can carry
and the rest of your team is to support that unit
so if you pick like, idk, lotan
u want people to funnel her aliemus, give her more strikes, multiply strike damage
that kind of thing
and other support functions that all teams need are weakness, vulnerable, and keyflare generation
once you have those sorted you have quite a lot of flexibility

The general advice for standard pulls is to go for Blade of the Titan until it hits +12.
Then Incalc until it hits +12.
This is called Wheel Jail.

Best team to clear Arc 2 and D-tide team suggestions? My current team is as attached
Also I was wondering if getting better covenants are essential? Because idk how to raise the percentage mark
You shouldn't worry too much about the covenant yet, but if you wanna complete D-tide and Arc 2 I have a few teams recs that will maybe work

For a Pollux main DPS team I suggest going with Aqueor realm as it's pretty helpful. My team for most of the hard gameplay rn is: Celeste (sig WoD rec), Pollux (sig rec with CRIT DMG focus), Castor (with at least enough alimeus regen to have his ult ready in 2 turns at least) and Tulu/Goliath/Caecus (they all work well, but I recommend Tulu with his sig WoD and alimeus regen focus as well)
This is just what I personally used since I leveled up a lot of my characters, but teams with Caro realm and Ultra realm work too
For Caro I recommend Thais and Pickman since they do well together, alongside the Divus twins
Gmurphy is good at E3 or do I need her over exalt?
And also can corospant (I think I misspelled her name) be used at E0 or do I need more?

From experience, over-exalt only slightly enhances character's ults and adds a new effect, so I'd say to get it from the monthly shop shard. As for corposant (yeah it's hard to spell I don't blame you), it's the same. Still, I would say to try to get a few more copies of it depending on how much you wanna build her
If it's for casual fights the WoD should be fine as it is for the time being until you can get more copies
